text:
There was once a village that had an unusual tradition. Every 100 years, the villagers held a grand festival. During this festival, everyone would forget everything they knew. Art, politics, even their own names! People became animals, animals became people, and animals had conversations.

It was during this unprecedented time that a tiny bluebird named Blümel discovered a peculiar talent. Whenever he sang about the forgotten arts and wisdom of the past, flowers blossomed. These flowers were ancient knowledge seeds, waiting to grow back into the wisdom lost to time.

Sooner than anyone realized, nature was filled with more knowledge than ever. Animals could tell stories they couldn there weren't human. Birds could play the lute. Even rocks knew secrets of the forest. That year, the village had no need to forget—the flowers sung to them.

And that year, a wise owl who could see the full moon gathered all the old tales under the light of a full moon. Those moonlit stories became the
